definition.text: A disease is a disposition to undergo pathological processes that exists in an organism because of one or more disorders in that organism.

An endocrine or hormonal disorder that occurs when the adrenal cortex does not produce enough of the hormone cortisol and in some cases, the hormone aldosterone. It may be due to a disorder of the adrenal cortex (Addison's disease or primary adrenal insufficiency) or to inadequate secretion of ACTH by the pituitary gland (secondary adrenal insufficiency).

name: colorblindness, partial

synonyms.text: genetic deficiency of early component of the classical complement pathway

definition.text: A genetic deficiency of any early component of the classical pathway (C1q, C1r/s, C2, C4, and C3) that is associated with autoimmune diseases due to the failure of clearance of immune complexes (IC) and apoptotic materials, and the impairment of normal humoral response.

comments: See genetic heterogeneity of OMIM 600631.
name: nocturnal enuresis

definition.text: Urination during sleep.

synonyms.text: fever-associated acute infantile liver failure syndrome

comments: OMIM series 615438. Plus one non-syndrome. OMIM series covers only syndromes but I left off "syndrome" from the series name (smb).
name: infantile liver failure

definition.text: An instance of sleep-related hypermotor epilepsy that is caused by an inherited genomic modification in an individual.

relationships.comment: Febrile seizure (within the age range of 3 months to 6 years)

definition.text: Hypophosphatemic rickets is a group of genetic diseases characterized by hypophosphatemia, rickets, and normal serum levels of calcium.

parents.comment: combined pituitary hormone deficiencies, genetic form

definition.text: A microphthalmia that is not part of a larger syndrome.

name: microvascular complications of diabetes, susceptibility

definition.text: A mitochondrial energy metabolism disorder where respiratory complex (I–V) is dysfunctional, typically due to mutations in genes encoding that specific complex’s proteins or assembly factors.

definition.text: A common presentation of craniosynostosis and polysyndactyly.

comments: Text of OMIM 613088 points to OMIM 176780 for disease description.
name: pelvic organ prolapse

definition.text: Abnormal descent of a pelvic organ resulting in the protrusion of the organ beyond its normal anatomical confines. Symptoms often include vaginal discomfort, dyspareunia; urinary stress incontinence; and fecal incontinence.
A developmental brain abnormality characterized by an excessive amount of small convolutions on the surface of the brain and cognitive dysfunction.
Unusually early sexual maturity.

synonyms.text: progressive external ophthalmoplegia with mtDNA deletions

name: progressive external ophthalmoplegia with mitochondrial DNA deletions

name: anemia, hypochromic microcytic with iron overload

name: anemia, nonspherocytic hemolytic

definition.text: Auriculo-condylar syndrome (ACS) presents with bilateral external ear malformations ('question mark' ears), mandibular condyle hypoplasia, microstomia, micrognathia, microglossia and facial asymmetry. Additional manifestations include hypotonia, ptosis, cleft palate, puffy cheeks, developmental delay, impaired hearing and respiratory distress.

intersection_of: predisposes_towards MONDO:0005229 ! bacterial infectious disease with sepsis

definition.text: Bifid nose is a rare congenital malformation of presumed autosomal dominant or recessive inheritance characterized by clefting of the nose ranging from a minimally noticeable groove in the columella to complete clefting of the underlying bones and cartilage (resulting in two half noses) with a usually adequate airway. Bifid nose may be seen in frontonasal dysplasia while other malformations such as hypertelorbitism and midline clefts of the lip may also be associated.

comments: Text for OMIM 211910 includes 211920 and 611929 as other types.
name: camptodactyly syndrome, Guadalajara

name: cerebelloparenchymal disorder

comments: Nomenclature of OMIM 118420 includes 207950 as another type.
name: Chiari malformation

definition.text: A rare genetic brain malformation characterized by displacement of the brain stem and cerebellum through the foramen magnum. It may result in hydrocephalus.

comments: Genetic heterogeneity of OMIM:306955 which includes CONGENITAL HEART DEFECTS, MULTIPLE TYPES, 1, X-LINKED lists 614980, 614954 and 615779 as forms of this disease.
name: congenital heart defects, multiple types

definition.text: Geleophysic dysplasia is a rare skeletal dysplasia characterized by short stature, prominent abnormalities in hands and feet, and a characteristic facial appearance (described as \

comments: See genetic heterogeneity of OMIM 256850.
name: giant axonal neuropathy

definition.text: A rare inherited disorder affecting the neurofilaments. It is caused by mutations in the GAN gene. It is characterized by the presence of abnormally large nerve cell axons. Signs and symptoms include difficulty walking, sensory disturbances, lack of motor coordination and abnormal reflexes in the limbs.

comments: Editor note: check relationship to multiple acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency
name: glutaric aciduria

definition.text: The Immunodeficiency, Centromeric region instability, Facial anomalies syndrome (ICF) is a rare autosomal recessive disease characterized by immunodeficiency, although B cells are present, and by characteristic rearrangements in the vicinity of the centromeres (the juxtacentromeric heterochromatin) of chromosomes 1 and 16 and sometimes 9.

comments: This term's classification was reviewed in the context of the Strategic Refinement project (2023) and was determined to be excluded from the 'disorder of visual system' (MONDO:0024458) ontology branch (https://orcid.org/0000-0001-9310-0163)
name: keratosis follicularis spinulosa decalvans

definition.text: Keratosis follicularis spinulosa decalvans is a rare genodermatosis occurring during infancy or childhood, predominantly affecting males, and characterized by diffuse follicular hyperkeratosis associated with progressive cicatricial alopecia of the scalp, eyebrows and eyelashes. Additional findings can include photophobia, corneal dystrophy, facial erythema, and/or palmoplantar keratoderma.
Mosaic variegated aneuploidy (MVA) syndrome is a chromosomal anomaly characterized by multiple mosaic aneuploidies that leads to a variety of phenotypic abnormalities and cancer predisposition.

comments: See genetic heterogeneity of OMIM 614389. RPRGL4 exists only as and INCLUDED entity of OMIM 604759.
name: pregnancy loss, recurrent, susceptibility

comments: See genetic heterogeneity of OMIM 614742.
name: pulmonary fibrosis and/or bone marrow failure, telomere-related

parents.comment: disorder of thiamine metabolism and transport

definition.text: A congenital cardiac defect in which two heart vessels are reversed (transposed).

rare inborn error of sequestering of triglyceride

comments: See text of OMIM 190430.
name: triglyceride storage disease

definition.text: An inherited metabolic disease that is has its basis in the disruption of sequestering of triglyceride.

A spectrum of hip abnormalities commonly presenting in infancy involving the relationship between the femoral head and the acetabulum and that includes subluxation or dislocation at rest or upon provocation.

comments: Genetic heterogeneity of OMIM 614675 includes 615715.
name: bone marrow failure syndrome

definition.text: An epilepsy syndrome characterized by adult-onset cortical myoclonus typically first seen as tremulous finger movements and myoclonus of the extremities.
A grouping for Huntington disease and similar diseases.
A developmental disorder of the eye characterized by unilateral or bilateral microphthalmia associated with ocular coloboma.

qualitative or quantitative defects of protein O-mannosyltransferase 2

definition.text: Neu-Laxova syndrome (NLS) is a rare, multiple malformation syndrome characterized by severe intrauterine growth retardation (IUGR), severe microcephaly with a sloping forehead, severe ichthyosis (collodion baby type), and facial dysmorphism.

comments: Note that OMIM has two PS that appear to refer to the same thing
name: microcephaly and chorioretinopathy

name: congenital myasthenic syndrome with tubular aggregates

definition.text: A congenital myasthenic syndrome with a finding of tubular aggregates in myofibers.

comments: Editor note: note that in contrast to other phenotypic series, all members are caused by the same gene, SLC2A1
name: GLUT1 deficiency syndrome

definition.text: An epileptic encephalopathy resulting from impaired glucose transport into the brain.

A disorder characterized by an electrocardiographic finding of a rapid grossly irregular ventricular rhythm with marked variability in QRS cycle length, morphology, and amplitude. The rate is typically greater than 300 bpm. (CDISC)

synonyms.text: 11-alpha beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type I deficiency of
hyperandrogenism due to cortisone reductase deficiency

definition.text: A disorder in which there is a failure to regenerate the active glucocorticoid cortisol from cortisone via 11beta-HSD1. The resulting lack of cortisol regeneration stimulates ACTH-mediated adrenal hyperandrogenism, with males manifesting in childhood with precocious pseudopuberty and females presenting in adolescence and early adulthood with hirsutism, oligoamenorrhea, and infertility.

synonyms.text: gingival fibromatosis, abnormal fingers, fingernails, nose and ears, and splenomegaly

definition.text: A rare disorder characterized by gingival fibromatosis, coarse facial appearance, and absence or hypoplasia of nails or terminal phalanges of hands and feet.

microcephaly, short stature, and impaired glucose metabolism 1

name: microcephaly, short stature, and impaired glucose metabolism 1

name: prenatal-onset spinal muscular atrophy with congenital bone fractures

name: striatal degeneration, autosomal dominant

definition.text: An adult-onset movement disorder characterized by bradykinesia, dysarthria and muscle rigidity.

synonyms.text: familial infantile hypercalcemia with suppressed intact parathyroid hormone

relationships.comment: obsolete nephropathy secondary to a storage or other metabolic disease

definition.text: A hypercalcemia disease that occurs between 28 days to one year of life.
Acute form of gonococcal seminal vesiculitis.

name: acquired carbohydrate metabolism disease

definition.text: An acquired metabolic disease that is has its basis in the disruption of carbohydrate metabolism.

synonyms.text: Human ehrlichial infection, human monocytic type
human ehrlichiosis caused by Ehrlichia chaffeensis
human ehrlichiosis due to Ehrlichia chaffeensis

definition.text: A form of ehrlichiosis associated with Ehrlichia chaffeensis, an obligate intracellular pathogen affecting monocytes and macrophages.
An infectious disease caused by infection with rickettsia conorii subsp. coronorii.

intersection_of: disease_has_infectious_agent NCBITaxon:317865 ! Rickettsia conorii subsp. indica

definition.text: An infectious disease caused by infection with rickettsia conorii subsp. israelensis.

intersection_of: disease_has_infectious_agent NCBITaxon:45258 ! Rickettsia conorii subsp. israelensis
disease_has_infectious_agent NCBITaxon:226665 ! Rickettsia conorii subsp. heilongjiangensis

definition.text: A spotted fever that has material basis in Rickettsia honei, which is transmitted by cayenne ticks (Amblyomma cajennense). The infection has symptom mild spotted fever, has symptom eschar and has symptom adenopathy.

A spotted fever that has material basis in Rickettsia japonica, which is transmitted by ticks (Dermacentor taiwanensis and Haemaphysalis flava). The infection has symptom fever, has symptom eschars, has symptom regional adenopathy, and has symptom rash on extremities.

A spotted fever that has material basis in Rickettsia parkeri, which is transmitted by Gulf Coast tick (Amblyomma maculatum). The infection has symptom fever, has symptom headache, has symptom eschar, and has symptom rash.
A anthrax infection that involves the oropharynx.
Adiaspiromycosis is a rare fungal infection in the lung and is caused by inhalation of spores of the saprophytic soil fungus Chrysosporium parvum var crescens (previously known as Emmonsia crescens).

An aspergillosis that is a serious fungal infection of the lung with pneumonia caused by Aspergillus, which spreads to other parts of the body through bloodstream in patients with acute leukemia and recipients of tissue transplants. Clinical symptoms include pulmonary nodules and hemorrhage.

synonyms.text: congestive cardiomyopathy due to selenium deficiency
enlarged heart and poor heart function

comments: The etiological mechanism of KD remains largely unclear, selenium and Coxsackie virus infection have been suggested to be highly involved in the pathogenesis of KD [PMID:33634251].
name: Keshan disease

definition.text: A congestive cardiomyopathy caused by a combination of dietary deficiency of selenium and the presence of a mutated strain of Coxsackievirus.

intersection_of: disease_disrupts GO:0001887 ! selenium compound metabolic process
MONDO:0100332 NCBITaxon:12066 ! disease has primary infectious agent Coxsackievirus

definition.text: A dermatophytosis that involves the beard.
A dermatophyte infection of the hair that infects the hair surface.
A dermatophyte infection of the hair that nvade the hair shaft and internalize into the hair cell.

A tinea corporis that results in fungal infection located in skin, has material basis in Trichophyton concentricum, which is characterized by ring-like growth in overlapping circles that may have an autosomal dominant genetic predisposition.

A dengue disease that involves the most severe form of dengue fever, has material basis in Dengue virus [NCBITaxon:12637] with four serotypes (Dengue virus 1, 2, 3 and 4), which are transmitted by Aedes mosquito bite. The infection has symptom easy bruising, has symptom blood spots, has symptom bleeding gums, and has symptom nosebleeds. It is accompanied by circulatory collapse, involves hypotension, narrow pulse pressure (less than or equal to 20mm Hg), or frank shock. The shock occurs after two to six days of symptoms, followed by collapse, weak pulse, and blueness around the mouth.
Watery voluminous diarrhea resulting from an imbalance between ion and water secretion and absorption.
A diarrhea that results from the presence of osmotically active, poorly absorbed solutes in the bowel lumen that inhibit normal water and electrolyte absorption.

synonyms.text: diarrhea secondary to altered bowel motility
diarrhoea secondary to altered bowel motility

comments: Reason of obsoletion: out of scope - MONDO:excludeHistoricalDisease. Term to consider: -
name: diarrheal disease secondary to altered bowel motility

definition.text: A diarrhea that results from either increased or decreased motility in the bowel.
An diarrhea (disease) involving a pathogenic inflammatory response in the intestinal mucosa.

Either of two diseases resulting from fungal infection of the hair shafts. Black piedra occurs mainly in and on the hairs of the scalp and is caused by Piedraia hortae; white piedra occurs in and on the hairs of the scalp, beard, mustache and genital areas and is caused by Trichosporon species.
